snapshot.scope: Independent Product Planning review retains the published Reader, Home conversation, Update, release, security and Settings contracts and binds the machine-local Skill Registry foundation on sole main a13c42e: checksum-bound pathless inventory, fail-closed manifests, revision-fenced disable and exact API/data/Skill/phase/trace ownership without renderer binding or lifecycle-status promotion.

reviewers.task: Machine-local Skill Registry foundation contract and governance review
reviewers.independence: Product Planning did not author the Development Skill Registry implementation and independently compared its exact bytes with Skill, API, settings, data, architecture, phase, trace, acceptance and permission owners on the latest published main.

dimensions.summary: The existing Skill Extension owner remains the lifecycle contract; API, architecture, data and settings owners receive bounded current-state facts without a duplicate Skill or package specification.

Skill, API, architecture, data and settings owners agree that only user-confirmed machine-local non-package manifests project, disable reduces authority, and absent lifecycle actions remain unavailable.

P8, PIGE-SKILL-001 and E8.01 remain planned; their targets record the bounded inventory/disable foundation while preserving staging, install, enable, update, uninstall, export, runtime, permissions and UI gaps.

Focused service/schema/shell tests cover checksum and identity binding, no-follow failures, strict pathless projections, atomic CAS disable and parsed main/preload IPC; repository gates remain directly runnable.
The synchronization is confined to existing Skill/API/architecture/data/settings/decision/phase/trace owners plus derived locks; no new owner document or duplicated lifecycle narrative was added.

verification.command: Independent review of the machine-local Skill Registry foundation mechanically replayed on sole main a13c42e
verification.evidence: Review confirms exact main-owned app-data layout, manifest checksum and identity binding, pathless/body-free summaries, invalid counting, atomic CAS disable and the absence of install, enable, uninstall, execution, network, process, package and grant authority.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions && npm run verify:traceability
verification.evidence: Links, whitespace, document map, PRD structure, decisions and locked Skill claims pass with no Requirement, Build, Exit or Phase promotion.

verification.command: npm run verify:contracts && npm run verify:independent-documentation-review && npm run verify:documentation-leanness
verification.evidence: CON-001 through CON-004 remain green; focused Skill tests, typecheck and build bind the exact service/contracts/IPC while the union recipe retains published Reader, Update, release, security and Settings roots.

coordination.compatibility: The candidate adds a fail-closed machine-local inventory and authority-reducing disable without renderer binding, Skill execution, package behavior, vault data, backup input, network access or permission-grant mutation.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning reviewed the exact Development bytes and synchronized only existing owners; this foundation does not claim staging, install, enable, update, uninstall, export, runtime use, Permission Broker integration or complete Skill lifecycle acceptance.

nonBlockingRisks: The renderer Settings surface is not bound to this API and must remain unavailable for absent actions.
Enable awaits Permission Broker authority; uninstall awaits explicit confirmation plus Skill-grant revocation; no Requirement, Exit or Phase status is promoted.
The published Update Service still has no production feed/download/install/restart path, and the Home conversation repair makes no unrelated Skill lifecycle claim.
